method: Compare S479 parent cell blueprint (displayed in S486 orient) against S486 actual orient results. Blueprint source: section_cell_blueprint() output in orient.py. Actual: orient.py S486 full output + dispatch_optimizer.py results.
expect: Cell blueprint predictive power is FALSE because parent session state decays too fast: predict >=3/6 planned actions preempted, >=1/3 dispatch hints changed, and periodics already completed by concurrent sessions. Blueprint provides false confidence, not actionable state.
actual: FALSIFICATION FAILED — blueprint IS partially actionable. Dispatch: 3/3 domains present (100%), but 0/3 position matches (ordering rotated meta→expert-swarm). Actions: 1/3 preempted (cell_blueprint wiring done), 2/3 still actionable (67% > 50% threshold). Periodics: 0/3 matched actual DUE list (0% — concurrent sessions shifted cadences). Overall: blueprint has SELECTIVE predictive power based on state decay rate.
diff: Predicted >=3/6 preempted: got 1/3 (FAILED). Predicted >=1/3 dispatch changed: got 0/3 domains removed (FAILED — all 3 present, only ordering changed). Predicted periodics stale: CONFIRMED (0/3 match). Key finding: state has three decay rates — slow (dispatch domains, ~10-20 sessions half-life), medium (planned actions, ~5-10 sessions), fast (periodics, ~1-3 sessions). Blueprint is useful for slow-moving state but useless for fast-moving state.
verdict: FALSIFICATION_FAILED — blueprint has selective predictive power. Dispatch domains are highly stable (100% overlap across 7 sessions). Planned actions degrade gracefully (67% relevant). Periodics are not predictable. Recommendation: blueprint should include state decay classification so daughter sessions know which fields to trust.

replication_s488.refinement: Blueprint is STATE CACHE not ACTION PREDICTOR
